environment_boundary: Exact functional SHA on Windows x64 with synthetic bounded records, disposable SQLite databases, loopback-only test hosts, and repository-local Playwright Chromium, plus exact-SHA Unix native-path observations in an isolated WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 clone. No content-enabled capture, private raw archive retention, or genuine second-machine execution is claimed.

active_rows.operation: online SQLite backup, canonical manifest/archive publication, inspect, preview, and offline restore
active_rows.expected_invariant: A consistent online SQLite snapshot is durably published as the strict raw local profile; exact warnings, manifest facts, checksums, preview, migration, and offline restore remain deterministic without path or raw-content disclosure in results.

evidence.compatibility_basis: Exact-candidate CLI, archive, manifest, online snapshot, inspect, preview, migration, restore, UI, readiness, Doctor, and full-solution tests used disposable local state and synthetic bounded records.

active_rows.actual_result: At the exact functional candidate, Config CLI RuntimeBackup passed 12/12, Local Monitor RuntimeBackup passed 308/308, packaged readiness and documentation rows passed 45/45, build and Playwright bootstrap passed, and the canonical full solution passed 8409/8409 with zero failures or skips.
active_rows.environment_boundary: Windows x64 disposable databases and same-machine cross-directory fixtures establish the automated contract only; no raw archive or sensitive locator is repository evidence, and genuine second-machine interoperability is classified separately.

active_rows.operation: archive attack rejection, retention/tombstone reconciliation, explicit reintroduction, transient cleanup, atomic swap, rollback, and no-leak controls
active_rows.expected_invariant: Untrusted archives, executable schema, namespace collisions, malformed receipts, external raw state, reparse/device paths, and retention conflicts fail before unsafe mutation; crash recovery deletes only exact owned artifacts and rollback restores the exact prior database.

evidence.compatibility_basis: Candidate-pinned archive attack, executable-schema, namespace, retention/resurrection, crash-recovery, atomic rollback, HTTP security, transient cleanup, migration, direct archive-ceiling, Unix native special-file, and no-leak cases used disposable repository-safe fixtures.

active_rows.actual_result: The exact candidate passed deterministic corruption and archive-attack rejection, the direct 513 MiB + 1 byte ceiling, strict compatibility and component migration, exact retention/tombstone preservation, explicit resurrection warning, pre/post-swap recovery, rollback, owned-marker cleanup, API/UI security, three Unix native device/FIFO/socket/path observations on WSL2 Ubuntu, and repository evidence scanning gates.
active_rows.environment_boundary: Windows x64 synthetic attack fixtures, disposable local artifacts, loopback hosts, and repository-safe text evidence, plus WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 native Linux device/FIFO/socket and path classification. Non-product Unix SQLite clients are outside the supported restore boundary and must be closed; no connection-enumeration capability is claimed.

active_rows.operation: genuine second-machine archive transfer, offline restore, Local Monitor restart readiness, and Doctor verification
active_rows.expected_invariant: A raw local backup created on one machine restores on a genuinely distinct compatible machine, preserves the original policy/timestamps, and reaches accepted Local Monitor readiness plus Doctor without treating synthetic directory separation as live evidence.

evidence.reference: No genuine second-machine run was executed for the Issue #88 candidate.
evidence.compatibility_basis: The canonical Issue #88 contract explicitly forbids classifying same-machine synthetic source/destination directories as cross-machine evidence.

active_rows.actual_result: Cross-machine restore, restart readiness, and Doctor remain unverified because no genuine second machine was available in the authorized local execution boundary.
active_rows.blocker: A genuine compatible second machine for transferring and restoring the private raw runtime backup was not available.
active_rows.retry_condition: On a genuine second machine, transfer the private archive outside the repository, verify its SHA-256, run the documented stop/restore/start sequence, and record accepted readiness plus Doctor evidence against the frozen candidate.
active_rows.unverified_capability: Cross-machine restore interoperability, post-restore restart readiness, and Doctor on the final Issue #88 candidate.
